title: TopoDiff: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Topology Optimization
abstract: We propose TopoDiff - a conditional diffusion-model-based architecture to perform performance-aware and manufacturability-aware topology optimization. Our model introduces a new guidance strategy to make diffusion models performance-aware and constraints-aware.
method_summary: First application of conditional diffusion models to topology optimization, outperforming GANs with a novel physics-guided sampling strategy that ensures structural performance and manufacturability.

title: Transformer-Based Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization enables the design of highly efficient and complex structures, but conventional iterative methods, such as SIMP-based approaches, often suffer from high computational costs and slow convergence.
method_summary: Applies transformer architecture to one-shot topology optimization, using attention mechanisms to capture long-range structural dependencies and generate optimal topologies without iterative FEA.

title: A Foundation Model for Shape- and Resolution-Free Structural Topology Optimization
abstract: Structural topology optimization (TO) is central to engineering design but remains computationally intensive due to complex physics and hard constraints. Existing deep-learning methods are limited to fixed resolutions and domain shapes.
method_summary: Proposes the first foundation model for topology optimization that generalizes across arbitrary domain shapes and resolutions, trained on diverse structural problems.

title: Physics-Informed Transformer for Real-Time High-Fidelity Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization is used for the design of high-performance structures but remains fundamentally limited by its iterative nature, requiring repeated finite element analyses that prevent real-time application.
method_summary: Combines physics-informed training with transformer architecture for real-time topology optimization, eliminating the need for iterative FEA during inference.

title: A Dual Physics-Informed Neural Network for Topology Optimization (DPNN-TO)
abstract: We propose a novel dual physics-informed neural network for topology optimization (DPNN-TO), which merges physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) with the traditional SIMP-based topology optimization. By embedding deep learning within the physical constraints, DPNN-TO eliminates the need for large-scale data and analytical sensitivity analysis.
method_summary: Integrates dual PINNs with SIMP-based topology optimization, using one network for displacement and another for density, eliminating need for training data and sensitivity analysis.

title: Gradient-Free Neural Topology Optimization
abstract: Gradient-free optimizers allow for tackling problems regardless of the smoothness or differentiability of their objective function, but they require many more iterations to converge when compared to gradient-based methods.
method_summary: Proposes a pre-trained neural reparameterization strategy that achieves order-of-magnitude iteration reduction for gradient-free topology optimization in latent space.

title: Latent Space Diffusion for Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization enables the automated design of efficient structures by optimally distributing material within a defined domain. However, traditional gradient-based methods often scale poorly with problem size.
method_summary: Applies diffusion models in a learned latent space for scalable topology optimization, decoupling the generative process from mesh resolution to handle large-scale 3D problems.

title: Accelerating Metamaterial Topology Optimization Using Deep Super-Resolution Networks
abstract: To address this challenge, we propose a novel deep learning framework for metamaterial topology optimization using an enhanced deep super-resolution (EDSR) approach. Generating low-resolution topological designs and upscaling them.
method_summary: Uses deep super-resolution networks to upscale low-resolution topology optimization results for metamaterials, dramatically reducing computational cost while preserving fine structural details.

title: Diverse Topology Optimization Using Modulated Neural Fields (TOM)
abstract: To address this limitation, we introduce Topology Optimization using Modulated Neural Fields (TOM) -- a data-free method that trains a neural network to generate structurally compliant shapes and explore diverse design alternatives.
method_summary: Uses modulated neural fields to generate diverse topology-optimized designs from a single training run, enabling design space exploration without pre-computed datasets.

title: GANTL: Towards Practical and Real-Time Topology Optimization with Conditional GANs and Transfer Learning
abstract: The proposed method combines the generative power of conditional GANs with the knowledge transfer capabilities of transfer learning methods to predict optimal topologies for unseen boundary conditions.
method_summary: Combines conditional GANs with transfer learning for real-time topology optimization, enabling rapid prediction of optimal structures for new boundary conditions without retraining.

title: Accelerated Topology Optimization Design of 3D Structures Based on Deep Learning
abstract: To train the neural network, the Simplified Isotropic Material with Penalization (SIMP) method was adopted to generate the dataset of the optimized structures under randomness loading conditions and volume fractions.
method_summary: Uses 3D encoder-decoder CNN trained on SIMP-generated datasets to directly predict 3D topology-optimized structures, achieving orders of magnitude speedup over iterative methods.

title: TOuNN: Topology Optimization using Neural Networks
abstract: Neural networks, and more broadly, machine learning techniques, have been recently exploited to accelerate topology optimization through data-driven training and image processing. In this paper, we demonstrate an alternate use of neural networks.
method_summary: Uses neural networks as continuous density field representations within the optimization loop, replacing discrete element-wise design variables with a smooth neural parameterization.

title: Multiphysics Design Optimization via Generative Adversarial Networks
abstract: This work presents a method for generating concept designs for coupled multiphysics problems by employing generative adversarial networks (GANs).
method_summary: Applies GANs to multi-physics topology optimization problems involving coupled thermal-structural objectives, generating designs that satisfy multiple physics constraints simultaneously.

title: Multi-Material Multi-Physics Topology Optimization with Physics-Informed Gaussian Process Priors
abstract: Machine learning (ML) has been increasingly used for topology optimization (TO). However, most existing ML-based approaches focus on simplified benchmark problems due to their high computational cost.
method_summary: Introduces physics-informed Gaussian process priors for multi-material, multi-physics topology optimization, scaling ML-based TO to realistic engineering problems.

title: Toward Large Language Model-Driven Symbolic Topology Optimisation for Rapid Structural Concept Generation in Manufacturable Design
venue: Journal of Manufacturing and Materials Processing
abstract: This work explores the use of large language models for symbolic topology optimization, enabling rapid generation of structural concepts that satisfy manufacturability constraints.
method_summary: Pioneers LLM-driven symbolic topology optimization where natural language specifications are translated into manufacturable structural designs through LLM reasoning and code generation.

title: A Multidisciplinary Design and Optimization (MDO) Agent Driven by Large Language Models
abstract: To accelerate mechanical design and enhance design quality and innovation, we present a Multidisciplinary Design and Optimization (MDO) Agent driven by Large Language Models (LLMs).
method_summary: Proposes an LLM-driven agent for multidisciplinary design optimization that orchestrates simulation tools, interprets results, and iteratively refines designs through natural language interaction.

title: Using Hand-Drawn Inputs for Diffusion-Based Topology Optimization
abstract: Topology optimization (TO) is employed in engineering to optimize structural performance while maximizing material efficiency. However, traditional TO methods incur significant computational and time costs.
method_summary: Enables intuitive topology optimization by conditioning diffusion models on hand-drawn sketches, bridging designer intent with automated structural optimization.

title: Two-Stage Multiobjective Topology Optimization Method via SwinUnet with Enhanced Generalization
abstract: Topology optimization is a critical tool for modern structural design, yet existing methods often prioritize single objectives (e.g., compliance minimization) and suffer from prohibitive computational costs.
method_summary: Proposes a two-stage approach using SwinUnet transformer for multi-objective topology optimization, achieving Pareto-optimal designs with enhanced generalization across problem instances.

title: Diffusion Models for Topology Optimization in 3D Printing Applications
abstract: In recent times, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and autoencoders have gained popularity as an alternative to traditional iterative topology optimization methods.
method_summary: Applies diffusion models to 3D-printing-aware topology optimization, generating structures that are both structurally optimal and directly manufacturable via additive manufacturing.

title: A Perspective on AI-Enhanced Problem Solving in Engineering: Generative AI and Optimization
abstract: While optimization shines at precisely identifying highly-optimal solutions, GenAI excels at inferring problem requirements, bridging solution domains, handling mixed data modalities, and rapidly generating diverse candidate solutions.
method_summary: Provides a framework for combining generative AI with traditional optimization in engineering design, identifying complementary strengths and proposing hybrid workflows.

title: Benchmarking Generative AI Against Bayesian Optimization for Constrained Multi-Objective Inverse Design
abstract: This paper investigates the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) as generative optimizers for solving constrained multi-objective regression tasks, specifically within the challenging domain of engineering inverse design.
method_summary: Benchmarks LLMs against Bayesian optimization for constrained multi-objective engineering design, revealing strengths and limitations of generative AI approaches for inverse design.

title: Towards Goal, Feasibility, and Diversity-Oriented Deep Generative Models in Design
abstract: Advancing DGMs in engineering design requires new training objectives which promote engineering design objectives. In this paper, we present the first Deep Generative Model that simultaneously optimizes for goal achievement, feasibility, and diversity.
method_summary: Proposes training objectives for deep generative models that simultaneously optimize design performance, physical feasibility, and solution diversity for engineering applications.

title: Towards Generative Design Using Optimal Transport for Shape Exploration and Solution Field Interpolation
abstract: We propose a generative design framework using optimal transport for shape exploration and solution field interpolation, enabling smooth navigation of the design space.
method_summary: Uses optimal transport theory for generative design, enabling continuous interpolation between structural shapes and their associated physical solution fields for design space exploration.

title: Generative AI-Based Prompt Evolution Engineering Design Optimization with Vision-Language Model
abstract: Engineering design optimization requires an efficient combination of a 3D shape representation, an optimization algorithm, and a design performance evaluation method, which is often computationally expensive.
method_summary: Proposes using vision-language models with evolutionary prompt optimization for engineering design, where natural language prompts guide 3D shape generation toward optimal performance.

title: Evolving Through the Looking Glass: Learning Improved Search Spaces with Variational Autoencoders
abstract: We show how generative machine learning can learn a representation corresponding to a valid region of search space, enabling optimizers to search in the new latent space and always find solutions that satisfy constraints.
method_summary: Uses VAEs to learn valid design spaces for engineering optimization, ensuring that evolutionary search in latent space always produces feasible designs satisfying manufacturing constraints.

title: Simultaneous and Meshfree Topology Optimization with Physics-Informed Gaussian Processes
abstract: Topology optimization (TO) provides a principled mathematical approach for optimizing the performance of a structure by designing its material spatial distribution in a pre-defined domain and subject to constraints.
method_summary: Proposes meshfree topology optimization using physics-informed Gaussian processes, simultaneously solving the state equation and optimizing the design without FEA discretization.

title: Multi-scale Topology Optimization using Neural Networks
abstract: We propose a new method for multi-scale topology optimization that seamlessly ensures compatibility across neighboring microstructure cells without the need for a catalog of unit cells and associated connectivity constraints. Our approach consists of a topology neural network that optimizes the microstructure shape and distribution across the design domain as a continuous field.
method_summary: Neural network-based multi-scale topology optimization that generates spatially varying microstructures as a continuous field, ensuring inter-cell compatibility without pre-computed unit cell catalogs. Published at SIGGRAPH.

title: Consistent Machine Learning for Topology Optimization with Microstructure-Dependent Neural Network Material Models
abstract: Additive manufacturing methods together with topology optimization have enabled the creation of multiscale structures with controlled spatially-varying material microstructure. This work presents a framework for designing multiscale heterogeneous structures by merging homogenization-based topology optimization with consistent neural network material models that enforce polyconvexity, objectivity, and thermodynamic consistency.
method_summary: Merges homogenization-based topology optimization with physics-consistent neural network material models (enforcing polyconvexity, objectivity, symmetry) for multiscale structure design with spatially varying microstructures.

title: Guiding Topology Optimization Diffusion with Human Preferences
abstract: Inspired by preference alignment techniques in generative modeling, our method learns to suppress unrealistic outputs by modulating the reverse diffusion trajectory using gradients of human-aligned reward models for topology optimization.
method_summary: Applies RLHF-style preference alignment to diffusion-based topology optimization, using human preference reward models to guide the generative process toward designs that match engineer expectations.

title: Agentic LLM Orchestration of Engineering Analysis in Product Development Design Practice
abstract: This paper presents an agentic LLM framework that orchestrates engineering analysis workflows in product development, including FEA setup, mesh generation, solver configuration, and result interpretation, through natural language interaction with CAE tools.
method_summary: Proposes an agentic LLM system that orchestrates end-to-end engineering analysis (FEA/CFD) in product development, automating CAE workflow from problem specification to result interpretation via natural language.

title: SimuAgent: An LLM-Based Simulink Modeling Assistant Enhanced with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We introduce SimuAgent, an LLM-powered modeling and simulation agent tailored for Simulink. SimuAgent replaces verbose XML with a concise, dictionary-style Python representation, dramatically cutting context length. Trained with reflection-guided reinforcement learning, it plans and executes modeling tasks with high accuracy.
method_summary: LLM agent for Simulink modeling that uses compact Python representations and reflection-guided RL training to automate block diagram construction, parameter tuning, and simulation execution.

title: Large Language Model-Empowered Next-Generation Computer-Aided Engineering
abstract: Software development has entered a new era where large language models now serve as general-purpose reasoning engines, enabling natural language interaction and transformative applications across engineering domains. This paper surveys how LLMs are reshaping computer-aided engineering including FEA automation, CFD workflow orchestration, and design optimization.
method_summary: Surveys the emerging paradigm of LLM-empowered CAE, covering automated FEA/CFD setup, natural language-driven simulation, intelligent post-processing, and the path toward autonomous engineering analysis agents.

title: Automating Data-Driven Modeling and Analysis for Engineering Applications using Large Language Model Agents
abstract: In this study, we propose an innovative pipeline utilizing Large Language Model agents to automate data-driven modeling and analysis, with emphasis on regression tasks for engineering applications including surrogate modeling and material property prediction.
method_summary: Proposes an LLM agent pipeline that automates the entire data-driven modeling workflow for engineering (data preprocessing, feature selection, model training, validation) through natural language instructions.

title: Fast and Accurate Bayesian Optimization with Pre-trained Transformers for Constrained Engineering Problems
abstract: Bayesian Optimization (BO) is a foundational strategy for efficiently handling black-box functions with many constraints and expensive evaluations. We propose using pre-trained transformer models as surrogate functions within BO, achieving faster convergence on constrained engineering design problems.
method_summary: Replaces traditional Gaussian process surrogates in Bayesian optimization with pre-trained transformers, achieving faster convergence on constrained engineering design problems with expensive evaluations.

title: Large Language Models for Combinatorial Optimization of Design Structure Matrix
abstract: In complex engineering systems, the dependencies between components create intricate coupling patterns that significantly impact development efficiency. We apply LLMs to optimize Design Structure Matrices, a fundamental tool in systems engineering for managing component interdependencies.
method_summary: Applies LLMs to combinatorial optimization of Design Structure Matrices in systems engineering, using natural language reasoning to reorder and cluster coupled engineering components for efficient development.
